document_summary: Sage Publications author profile of Dr. Milton J. Bennett, co-founder of the Intercultural Communication Institute and director of the Intercultural Development Research Institute. Bennett holds a Ph.D. from the University of Minnesota in intercultural communication and sociology. He was a keynote speaker at the 11th China Communication Conference where Liu won his Excellent Paper Award. Profile documents Bennett's academic credentials, institutional roles, and scholarly contributions including the Developmental Model of Intercultural Sensitivity. Serves as peer achievement evidence for the conference's caliber.

snippets.text: Dr. Milton Bennett (co- editor) is co- founder of the Intercultural Communication Institute and director of the Intercultural Development Research Institute. His Ph.D. is from the University of Minnesota in intercultural communication and sociology, and his current research interests are in empathy and consciousness. For fifteen years Milton was on the faculty of the department of speech communication at Portland State University, where he taught courses in intercultural communication and consciousness studies. Now he develops and conducts intercultural training for corporations and universities in both domestic and global diversity, and he serves as the director of graduate study for the ICI/University of the Pacific Master of Arts in Intercultural Relations program. Milton created the Developmental Model of Intercultural Sensitivity, is the co- author (with Ed Stewart) of the revised edition of American Cultural Patterns, and is editor of Basic Concepts of Intercultural Communication
snippets.subject: Milton J. Bennett

Demonstrates the caliber of scholars who attended the same conference as Liu.
